ru.title: Парсинг SQL без предварительной загрузки схемы через верификацию и обучение с подкреплением
ru.desc: В работе предложен TRUST-SQL — метод для парсинга текста в SQL-запросы в условиях неизвестной схемы базы данных. Система использует четырёхфазный протокол, позволяющий агенту активно определять и верифицировать только релевантные таблицы из больших шумных метаданных. Авторы разработали оригинальную стратегию Dual-Track GRPO с маскированием преимуществ на уровне токенов, которая решает проблему приписания кредита путём разделения reward-ов за исследование и исполнение. Модель достигает улучшения на 30,6% и 16,6% для вариантов 4B и 8B соответственно и сравнивается с базовыми решениями, использующими полную схему.

en.title: Empowering SQL Parsing with Unknown Schemas
en.desc: TRUST-SQL is a novel approach to text-to-SQL parsing that tackles the challenge of unknown database schemas in real-world applications. It employs a four-phase protocol and a dual-track Generalized Reward Policy Optimization (GRPO) strategy to enhance credit assignment during the parsing process. By treating the task as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process, it allows the agent to dynamically identify and verify relevant metadata instead of relying on a complete schema. The results show significant improvements in performance, achieving up to 30.6% better accuracy compared to traditional methods that use pre-loaded metadata.

zh.title: TRUST-SQL：未知模式下的智能 SQL 解析
zh.desc: TRUST-SQL 是一种针对未知模式的文本到 SQL 解析方法，采用四阶段协议和双轨 GRPO 策略来改善信用分配。该方法不依赖于预加载的元数据，而是通过主动识别和验证相关子集来应对真实环境中的复杂数据库。我们将任务建模为部分可观察的马尔可夫决策过程，利用结构化的协议来确保推理基于经过验证的元数据。实验结果表明，TRUST-SQL 在多个基准测试中显著提高了性能，展示了其在没有预加载元数据的情况下仍能超越强基线的能力。

papers.title: WorldCam: Interactive Autoregressive 3D Gaming Worlds with Camera Pose as a Unifying Geometric Representation
papers.abstract: Abstract Video diffusion transformers enhanced with camera pose representation enable precise action control and long-term 3D consistency in interactive gaming environments through physics-based action spaces and geometric grounding.  					AI-generated summary Recent advances in video diffusion transformers have enabled interactive gaming world models that allow users to explore generated environments over extended horizons. However, existing approaches struggle with precise action control and long-horizon 3D consistency. Most prior works treat user actions as abstract conditioning signals, overlooking the fundamental geometric coupling between actions and the 3D world, whereby actions induce relative camera motions that accumulate into a global camera pose within a 3D world. In this paper, we establish camera pose as a unifying geometric representation to jointly ground immediate action control and long-term 3D consistency. First, we define a physics-based continuous action space and represent user inputs in the Lie algebra to derive precise 6-DoF camera poses, which are injected into the generative model via a camera embedder to ensure accurate action alignment. Second, we use global camera poses as spatial indices to retrieve relevant past observations, enabling geometrically consistent revisiting of locations during long-horizon navigation. To support this research, we introduce a large-scale dataset comprising 3,000 minutes of authentic human gameplay annotated with camera trajectories and textual descriptions. Extensive experiments show that our approach substantially outperforms state-of-the-art interactive gaming world models in action controllability, long-horizon visual quality, and 3D spatial consistency.

ru.title: Поза камеры как геометрический якорь для управления действиями и 3D-консистентности в генеративных игровых мирах
ru.desc: Статья представляет видеодиффузионные трансформеры, улучшенные представлением позы камеры, для создания интерактивных игровых сцен. Авторы используют физически обоснованное пространство действий и представление в алгебре Ли для получения точных 6-степеневых свободы поз камеры, которые встраиваются в генеративную модель. Глобальные позы камеры служат пространственными индексами для извлечения релевантных прошлых наблюдений, обеспечивая геометрически согласованную навигацию на длинных горизонтах. Авторы представили крупномасштабный датасет с 3000 минутами игрового процесса, аннотированного траекториями камеры и текстовыми описаниями.

en.title: Enhancing Gaming with Camera Pose for Better Action Control and 3D Consistency
en.desc: This paper presents a novel approach to enhance video diffusion transformers for interactive gaming by incorporating camera pose representation. By treating camera pose as a geometric link between user actions and the 3D environment, the authors improve action control and maintain long-term consistency in gameplay. They introduce a physics-based action space and utilize Lie algebra to derive precise camera poses, which are integrated into the generative model for better alignment of actions. Additionally, a large-scale dataset of human gameplay is provided to support their findings, demonstrating significant improvements over existing models in terms of action controllability and visual quality.

zh.title: 相机姿态：实现精确动作与3D一致性的关键
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种视频扩散变换器，结合相机姿态表示，能够在互动游戏环境中实现精确的动作控制和长期的3D一致性。通过物理基础的动作空间和几何基础，用户可以在生成的环境中进行探索。我们将相机姿态作为统一的几何表示，确保即时动作控制与长期3D一致性相结合。实验结果表明，该方法在动作可控性、长时间视觉质量和3D空间一致性方面显著优于现有的互动游戏世界模型。

papers.title: Thinking in Uncertainty: Mitigating Hallucinations in MLRMs with Latent Entropy-Aware Decoding
papers.abstract: Abstract  Recent advancements in multimodal large reasoning models (MLRMs) have significantly improved performance in visual question answering. However, we observe that transition words (e.g., because, however, and wait) are closely associated with hallucinations and tend to exhibit high-entropy states. We argue that adequate contextual reasoning information can be directly extracted from the token probability distribution. Inspired by superposed representation theory, we propose leveraging latent superposed reasoning to integrate multiple candidate semantics and maintain latent reasoning trajectories. The hypothesis is that reliance on discrete textual inputs may drive the model toward sequential explicit reasoning, underutilizing dense contextual cues during high-entropy reasoning stages. Therefore, we propose constructing rich semantic representations from the token probability distributions to enhance in-context reasoning. With this goal, we present Latent Entropy-Aware Decoding (LEAD), an efficient plug-and-play decoding strategy that leverages semantic context to achieve reliable reasoning. The heart of our method lies in entropy-aware reasoning mode switching. The model employs probability-weighted continuous embeddings under high-entropy states and transitions back to discrete token embeddings as entropy decreases. Moreover, we propose a prior-guided visual anchor injection strategy that encourages the model to focus on visual information. Extensive experiments show that LEAD effectively mitigates hallucinations across various MLRMs on multiple benchmarks.

ru.title: Избежать галлюцинаций через осведомлённое о энтропии декодирование с латентными рассуждениями
ru.desc: Работа посвящена решению проблемы галлюцинаций в многомодальных больших моделях рассуждения при ответах на визуальные вопросы. Авторы заметили, что переходные слова часто связаны с галлюцинациями и характеризуются высокой энтропией, что указывает на неуверенность модели. Они предлагают извлекать контекстную информацию напрямую из распределения вероятностей токенов, используя суперпозиционные представления для интеграции нескольких семантических кандидатов. Основной вклад — метод LEAD, стратегия декодирования, которая адаптивно переключается между непрерывными вероятностными представлениями при высокой энтропии и дискретными токенами при низкой энтропии, сохраняя при этом якорные визуальные признаки.

en.title: Enhancing Visual Reasoning with Latent Entropy-Aware Decoding
en.desc: This paper discusses improvements in multimodal large reasoning models (MLRMs) for visual question answering, particularly addressing the issue of hallucinations caused by transition words. The authors propose a new method called Latent Entropy-Aware Decoding (LEAD), which enhances reasoning by utilizing token probability distributions to create richer semantic representations. By switching between probability-weighted continuous embeddings and discrete token embeddings based on entropy levels, the model can better manage high-entropy reasoning situations. Additionally, a visual anchor injection strategy is introduced to help the model focus on relevant visual information, leading to reduced hallucinations in MLRMs across various benchmarks.

zh.title: 提升推理能力，减少幻觉现象
zh.desc: 最近，多模态大规模推理模型（MLRM）在视觉问答方面取得了显著进展。然而，我们发现过渡词（如因为、然而和等待）与幻觉密切相关，并且往往表现出高熵状态。我们提出通过潜在叠加推理来整合多个候选语义，并保持潜在推理轨迹，以增强上下文推理。我们的方法是构建丰富的语义表示，从而提高模型在高熵推理阶段的可靠性。

papers.title: Online Experiential Learning for Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Online Experiential Learning enables continuous improvement of language models through deployment experience by extracting and consolidating experiential knowledge via on-policy distillation.  					AI-generated summary The prevailing paradigm for improving large language models relies on offline training with human annotations or simulated environments, leaving the rich experience accumulated during real-world deployment entirely unexploited. We propose Online Experiential Learning (OEL), a framework that enables language models to continuously improve from their own deployment experience. OEL operates in two stages: first, transferable experiential knowledge is extracted and accumulated from interaction trajectories collected on the user side; second, this knowledge is consolidated into model parameters via on-policy context distillation, requiring no access to the user-side environment. The two stages are iterated to form an online learning loop, where the improved model collects higher-quality trajectories that yield richer experiential knowledge for subsequent rounds. We evaluate OEL on text-based game environments across multiple model scales and both thinking and non-thinking variants. OEL achieves consistent improvements over successive iterations, enhancing both task accuracy and token efficiency while preserving out-of-distribution performance. Our analysis further shows that extracted experiential knowledge is significantly more effective than raw trajectories, and that on-policy consistency between the knowledge source and the policy model is critical for effective learning.

ru.title: Обучение языковых моделей из реального опыта развёртывания через on-policy дистилляцию
ru.desc: В этой работе предлагается метод Online Experiential Learning (OEL), который позволяет языковым моделям непрерывно улучшаться на основе опыта, накопленного во время реального развёртывания. Фреймворк работает в два этапа: сначала извлекается и накапливается передаваемое знание из траекторий взаимодействия пользователей, затем это знание консолидируется в параметры модели через on-policy дистилляцию контекста без доступа к окружению пользователей. Два этапа итеративно повторяются, формируя цикл онлайн-обучения, где улучшенная модель собирает траектории более высокого качества. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что OEL обеспечивает последовательное улучшение точности и эффективности токенов на текстовых играх, при этом извлечённое знание значительно эффективнее сырых траекторий.

en.title: Continuous Learning from Real-World Experience
en.desc: Online Experiential Learning (OEL) is a new framework designed to enhance language models by leveraging their real-world deployment experiences. It consists of two main stages: first, it gathers valuable knowledge from user interactions, and then it refines the model parameters using this knowledge through on-policy distillation. This process creates a continuous learning loop, allowing the model to improve iteratively by collecting better interaction data over time. OEL has been tested in text-based games, showing significant improvements in task accuracy and efficiency while maintaining performance on unseen data.

zh.title: 在线体验学习：持续改进语言模型的创新方法
zh.desc: 在线体验学习（OEL）是一种新框架，旨在通过模型在实际部署中的经验不断改进语言模型。该方法分为两个阶段：首先，从用户交互中提取和积累可转移的经验知识；其次，通过在线策略蒸馏将这些知识整合到模型参数中。OEL形成一个在线学习循环，改进后的模型能够收集更高质量的交互轨迹，从而为后续迭代提供更丰富的经验知识。实验表明，OEL在多个文本游戏环境中表现出一致的性能提升，增强了任务准确性和令牌效率。

papers.title: FinToolBench: Evaluating LLM Agents for Real-World Financial Tool Use
papers.abstract: Abstract FinToolBench presents the first real-world benchmark for evaluating financial tool learning agents, featuring 760 executable tools and comprehensive evaluation criteria beyond simple execution success.  					AI-generated summary The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into the financial domain is driving a paradigm shift from passive information retrieval to dynamic, agentic interaction. While general-purpose tool learning has witnessed a surge in benchmarks, the financial sector, characterized by high stakes, strict compliance, and rapid data volatility, remains critically underserved. Existing financial evaluations predominantly focus on static textual analysis or document-based QA, ignoring the complex reality of tool execution. Conversely, general tool benchmarks lack the domain-specific rigor required for finance, often relying on toy environments or a negligible number of financial APIs. To bridge this gap, we introduce FinToolBench, the first real-world, runnable benchmark dedicated to evaluating financial tool learning agents. Unlike prior works limited to a handful of mock tools, FinToolBench establishes a realistic ecosystem coupling 760 executable financial tools with 295 rigorous, tool-required queries. We propose a novel evaluation framework that goes beyond binary execution success, assessing agents on finance-critical dimensions: timeliness, intent type, and regulatory domain alignment. Furthermore, we present FATR, a finance-aware tool retrieval and reasoning baseline that enhances stability and compliance. By providing the first testbed for auditable, agentic financial execution, FinToolBench sets a new standard for trustworthy AI in finance. The tool manifest, execution environment, and evaluation code will be open-sourced to facilitate future research.

ru.title: Реальный бенчмарк для оценки финансовых агентов с поддержкой инструментов
ru.desc: FinToolBench представляет первый реальный бенчмарк для оценки финансовых агентов, способных работать с инструментами, включающий 760 исполняемых финансовых инструментов и 295 специализированных задач. В отличие от существующих оценок, которые сосредоточены на статическом анализе текстов или общих бенчмарках с игрушечными окружениями, этот бенчмарк предлагает реалистичную экосистему с реальными финансовыми API. Авторы предлагают инновационную систему оценки, которая выходит за рамки простой проверки успешного выполнения и оценивает агентов по критически важным для финансов параметрам: своевременность, тип намерения и соответствие нормативно-правовой базе. Также представлен базовый метод FATR, использующий финансовую осведомленность для улучшения стабильности и соответствия требованиям регулирования при извлечении инструментов.

en.title: FinToolBench: Elevating Financial AI Evaluation Standards
en.desc: FinToolBench is a groundbreaking benchmark designed specifically for evaluating financial tool learning agents, featuring 760 executable tools and a comprehensive set of evaluation criteria. This benchmark addresses the unique challenges of the financial sector, which requires more than just simple execution success, by incorporating dimensions like timeliness and regulatory compliance. Unlike previous benchmarks that focused on static analysis or limited mock tools, FinToolBench provides a realistic environment for testing agents in dynamic financial scenarios. Additionally, it introduces FATR, a baseline for finance-aware tool retrieval and reasoning, enhancing the stability and compliance of AI in financial applications.

zh.title: 金融工具学习的全新基准
zh.desc: FinToolBench是第一个用于评估金融工具学习代理的真实世界基准，包含760个可执行工具和全面的评估标准。该基准超越了简单的执行成功，关注金融领域的动态交互和合规性。与现有的静态文本分析或文档问答评估不同，FinToolBench提供了一个真实的生态系统，结合了严格的工具查询和执行要求。通过引入FATR基线，FinToolBench为金融执行提供了可审计的测试平台，推动了金融领域可信AI的新标准。

papers.title: MiroThinker-1.7 & H1: Towards Heavy-Duty Research Agents via Verification
papers.abstract: Abstract MiroThinker-1.7 and MiroThinker-H1 are research agents that enhance complex reasoning through structured planning, contextual reasoning, and tool interaction, with MiroThinker-H1 incorporating verification at local and global levels for more reliable multi-step problem solving.  					AI-generated summary We present MiroThinker-1.7, a new research agent designed for complex long-horizon reasoning tasks. Building on this foundation, we further introduce MiroThinker-H1, which extends the agent with heavy-duty reasoning capabilities for more reliable multi-step problem solving. In particular, MiroThinker-1.7 improves the reliability of each interaction step through an agentic mid-training stage that emphasizes structured planning, contextual reasoning, and tool interaction. This enables more effective multi-step interaction and sustained reasoning across complex tasks. MiroThinker-H1 further incorporates verification directly into the reasoning process at both local and global levels. Intermediate reasoning decisions can be evaluated and refined during inference, while the overall reasoning trajectory is audited to ensure that final answers are supported by coherent chains of evidence. Across benchmarks covering open-web research, scientific reasoning, and financial analysis, MiroThinker-H1 achieves state-of-the-art performance on deep research tasks while maintaining strong results on specialized domains. We also release MiroThinker-1.7 and MiroThinker-1.7-mini as open-source models, providing competitive research-agent capabilities with significantly improved efficiency.

ru.title: Агенты с проверяемым мышлением для надежного многоэтапного рассуждения
ru.desc: В статье представлены исследовательские агенты MiroThinker-1.7 и MiroThinker-H1, которые улучшают способность к сложному многоэтапному рассуждению через структурированное планирование, контекстное понимание и взаимодействие с внешними инструментами. MiroThinker-H1 дополнительно включает механизмы верификации на локальном и глобальном уровнях, что позволяет оценивать промежуточные решения и проверять согласованность цепочки доказательств. Агенты обучаются специальной процедурой fine-tuning, которая повышает надежность каждого шага взаимодействия при решении сложных задач. Модели достигают передовых результатов на бенчмарках научных рассуждений, веб-исследований и финансового анализа, при этом открытые версии обеспечивают эффективность с конкурентными возможностями.

en.title: Enhancing Complex Reasoning with MiroThinker Agents
en.desc: MiroThinker-1.7 and MiroThinker-H1 are advanced research agents designed to tackle complex reasoning tasks through structured planning and contextual understanding. MiroThinker-1.7 enhances the reliability of multi-step problem solving by focusing on effective tool interaction and mid-training evaluations. MiroThinker-H1 builds on this by integrating verification processes that assess reasoning at both local and global levels, ensuring that each decision is supported by solid evidence. These agents have demonstrated state-of-the-art performance across various benchmarks, making them valuable tools for deep research in diverse fields.

zh.title: 增强复杂推理的智能代理
zh.desc: MiroThinker-1.7和MiroThinker-H1是用于增强复杂推理的研究代理。MiroThinker-1.7通过结构化规划、上下文推理和工具交互来提高每一步的可靠性。MiroThinker-H1在此基础上增加了局部和全局验证功能，使多步骤问题解决更加可靠。该模型在开放网络研究、科学推理和金融分析等基准测试中表现出色，同时保持在专业领域的强劲结果。

papers.title: AgentProcessBench: Diagnosing Step-Level Process Quality in Tool-Using Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract AgentProcessBench introduces a benchmark for evaluating step-level effectiveness in tool-augmented agent interactions, featuring diverse trajectories with detailed human annotations to improve process-level understanding and model performance.  					AI-generated summary While Large Language Models (LLMs) have evolved into tool-using agents, they remain brittle in long-horizon interactions. Unlike mathematical reasoning where errors are often rectifiable via backtracking, tool-use failures frequently induce irreversible side effects, making accurate step-level verification critical. However, existing process-level benchmarks are predominantly confined to closed-world mathematical domains, failing to capture the dynamic and open-ended nature of tool execution. To bridge this gap, we introduce AgentProcessBench, the first benchmark dedicated to evaluating step-level effectiveness in realistic, tool-augmented trajectories. The benchmark comprises 1,000 diverse trajectories and 8,509 human-labeled step annotations with 89.1% inter-annotator agreement. It features a ternary labeling scheme to capture exploration and an error propagation rule to reduce labeling ambiguity. Extensive experiments reveal key insights: (1) weaker policy models exhibit inflated ratios of correct steps due to early termination; (2) distinguishing neutral and erroneous actions remains a significant challenge for current models; and (3) process-derived signals provide complementary value to outcome supervision, significantly enhancing test-time scaling. We hope AgentProcessBench can foster future research in reward models and pave the way toward general agents. The code and data are available at https://github.com/RUCBM/AgentProcessBench.

ru.title: Пошаговая оценка надежности инструмент-ориентированных агентов
ru.desc: AgentProcessBench представляет собой первый бенчмарк для оценки качества каждого шага в длинных последовательностях действий агентов, использующих инструменты. Бенчмарк содержит 1000 различных траекторий с 8509 человеческими аннотациями на уровне отдельных шагов, что позволяет более точно оценивать ошибки в процессе выполнения. Исследование показывает, что современные модели испытывают сложности в различении нейтральных и ошибочных действий, а сигналы обратной связи на уровне процесса значительно улучшают масштабируемость при инференсе. Полученные результаты открывают новые направления для разработки reward моделей и создания более надежных агентов.

en.title: Evaluating Step-Level Effectiveness in Tool-Using Agents
en.desc: AgentProcessBench is a new benchmark designed to assess how well tool-using agents perform at each step of their tasks. It includes 1,000 varied task trajectories and over 8,500 human-annotated steps to help improve understanding and performance of these agents. The benchmark addresses the challenges of evaluating agents in open-ended scenarios, where mistakes can have lasting effects, unlike in closed mathematical problems. By providing detailed annotations and a unique labeling system, it aims to enhance the development of more robust AI models that can effectively use tools in complex environments.

zh.title: 提升工具增强代理的逐步有效性
zh.desc: AgentProcessBench是一个用于评估工具增强代理交互中逐步有效性的基准测试。它包含1000条多样化的轨迹和8509个经过人类标注的步骤注释，旨在提高过程级理解和模型性能。该基准采用三元标记方案来捕捉探索行为，并通过错误传播规则减少标注歧义。我们的实验表明，当前模型在区分中性和错误行为方面仍面临重大挑战，AgentProcessBench有望推动奖励模型的未来研究。

papers.title: SocialOmni: Benchmarking Audio-Visual Social Interactivity in Omni Models
papers.abstract: Abstract SocialOmni presents a benchmark for evaluating social interactivity in omni-modal large language models across speaker identification, interruption timing, and natural interruption generation, revealing gaps between perceptual accuracy and conversational competence.  					AI-generated summary Omni-modal large language models (OLMs) redefine human-machine interaction by natively integrating audio, vision, and text. However, existing OLM benchmarks remain anchored to static, accuracy-centric tasks, leaving a critical gap in assessing social interactivity, the fundamental capacity to navigate dynamic cues in natural dialogues. To this end, we propose SocialOmni, a comprehensive benchmark that operationalizes the evaluation of this conversational interactivity across three core dimensions: (i) speaker separation and identification (who is speaking), (ii) interruption timing control (when to interject), and (iii) natural interruption generation (how to phrase the interruption). SocialOmni features 2,000 perception samples and a quality-controlled diagnostic set of 209 interaction-generation instances with strict temporal and contextual constraints, complemented by controlled audio-visual inconsistency scenarios to test model robustness. We benchmarked 12 leading OLMs, which uncovers significant variance in their social-interaction capabilities across models. Furthermore, our analysis reveals a pronounced decoupling between a model's perceptual accuracy and its ability to generate contextually appropriate interruptions, indicating that understanding-centric metrics alone are insufficient to characterize conversational social competence. More encouragingly, these diagnostics from SocialOmni yield actionable signals for bridging the perception-interaction divide in future OLMs.
affiliations: Department of Computer Science, University of Rochester
Institute of Artificial Intelligence, Xiamen University
Media Analytics and Computing Lab, Xiamen University

ru.title: Социальная интерактивность омни-модальных LLM: от восприятия к диалогу
ru.desc: В статье представлен бенчмарк SocialOmni для оценки социальной интерактивности омни-модальных больших языковых моделей, которые одновременно обрабатывают аудио, видео и текст. Бенчмарк оценивает три ключевых аспекта: идентификацию говорящего, выбор момента для прерывания собеседника и качество генерации самого прерывания. Авторы показали, что существует значительный разрыв между точностью восприятия модели и её способностью генерировать контекстуально уместные реплики в динамичном диалоге. Результаты анализа 12 передовых омни-модальных моделей демонстрируют, что метрики понимания недостаточны для характеристики социальной компетентности в разговоре.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Evaluating Social Interactivity in Language Models
en.desc: The paper introduces SocialOmni, a new benchmark designed to evaluate social interactivity in omni-modal large language models (OLMs). It focuses on three key aspects: identifying speakers, timing interruptions, and generating natural interruptions during conversations. The study reveals that while some models perform well in understanding speech, they often struggle with generating appropriate conversational responses. This highlights the need for more comprehensive metrics that assess both perception and interaction capabilities in OLMs.

zh.title: 提升全模态语言模型的社交互动能力
zh.desc: SocialOmni是一个用于评估全模态大型语言模型社交互动能力的基准，主要关注说话者识别、打断时机和自然打断生成。现有的全模态语言模型基准主要集中在静态的准确性任务上，未能有效评估动态对话中的社交互动能力。通过对12000个样本的测试，SocialOmni揭示了不同模型在社交互动能力上的显著差异，并指出感知准确性与生成适当打断之间存在明显脱节。该基准为未来的全模态语言模型提供了改进社交互动能力的可行信号。

papers.title: M^3: Dense Matching Meets Multi-View Foundation Models for Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-view foundation model enhanced with matching head and monocular Gaussian splatting SLAM achieves improved pose estimation and scene reconstruction accuracy.  					AI-generated summary Streaming reconstruction from uncalibrated monocular video remains challenging, as it requires both high-precision pose estimation and computationally efficient online refinement in dynamic environments. While coupling 3D foundation models with SLAM frameworks is a promising paradigm, a critical bottleneck persists: most multi-view foundation models estimate poses in a feed-forward manner, yielding pixel-level correspondences that lack the requisite precision for rigorous geometric optimization. To address this, we present M^3, which augments the Multi-view foundation model with a dedicated Matching head to facilitate fine-grained dense correspondences and integrates it into a robust Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM. M^3 further enhances tracking stability by incorporating dynamic area suppression and cross-inference intrinsic alignment. Extensive experiments on diverse indoor and outdoor benchmarks demonstrate state-of-the-art accuracy in both pose estimation and scene reconstruction. Notably, M^3 reduces ATE RMSE by 64.3% compared to VGGT-SLAM 2.0 and outperforms ARTDECO by 2.11 dB in PSNR on the ScanNet++ dataset.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Сопоставление признаков в foundation models для точного SLAM из монокулярного видео
ru.desc: Авторы предлагают M³ — систему для одновременной локализации и построения карты (SLAM), которая объединяет многовидовую foundation model с специализированной головкой сопоставления для получения высокоточных плотных соответствий между кадрами. Интеграция этого модуля в монокулярное Gaussian Splatting позволяет эффективно восстанавливать трёхмерную геометрию сцены в реальном времени с одной видеокамеры. Дополнительно используются техники динамического подавления и кросс-инференс выравнивания параметров камеры для повышения стабильности отслеживания позы. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение точности оценки позы и реконструкции сцены по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: M^3: Enhancing Pose Estimation with Multi-view Models and SLAM
en.desc: This paper introduces M^3, a novel approach that combines a Multi-view foundation model with a Matching head and Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM to enhance pose estimation and scene reconstruction. The Matching head improves the accuracy of pixel-level correspondences, which are crucial for precise geometric optimization in dynamic environments. By integrating dynamic area suppression and cross-inference intrinsic alignment, M^3 achieves greater tracking stability. Experimental results show that M^3 significantly outperforms existing methods, reducing average translation error and improving scene reconstruction quality.

zh.title: 提升姿态估计与场景重建的M^3模型
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为M^3的多视角基础模型，结合了匹配头和单目高斯点云SLAM，以提高姿态估计和场景重建的准确性。该模型通过细致的密集对应关系，解决了传统多视角模型在几何优化中精度不足的问题。M^3还通过动态区域抑制和交叉推理内在对齐，增强了跟踪的稳定性。实验结果表明，M^3在姿态估计和场景重建方面的表现优于现有方法，显著降低了绝对轨迹误差。

papers.title: One-Eval: An Agentic System for Automated and Traceable LLM Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract One-Eval is an agentic evaluation system that automates large language model assessment by converting natural-language requests into executable workflows with integrated benchmark planning, dataset handling, and customizable reporting.  					AI-generated summary Reliable evaluation is essential for developing and deploying large language models, yet in practice it often requires substantial manual effort: practitioners must identify appropriate benchmarks, reproduce heterogeneous evaluation codebases, configure dataset schema mappings, and interpret aggregated metrics. To address these challenges, we present One-Eval, an agentic evaluation system that converts natural-language evaluation requests into executable, traceable, and customizable evaluation workflows. One-Eval integrates (i) NL2Bench for intent structuring and personalized benchmark planning, (ii) BenchResolve for benchmark resolution, automatic dataset acquisition, and schema normalization to ensure executability, and (iii) Metrics \& Reporting for task-aware metric selection and decision-oriented reporting beyond scalar scores. The system further incorporates human-in-the-loop checkpoints for review, editing, and rollback, while preserving sample evidence trails for debugging and auditability. Experiments show that One-Eval can execute end-to-end evaluations from diverse natural-language requests with minimal user effort, supporting more efficient and reproducible evaluation in industrial settings. Our framework is publicly available at https://github.com/OpenDCAI/One-Eval.
affiliations: Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications

ru.title: От текста к тестированию: агентная система для автоматической оценки LLM
ru.desc: One-Eval — это агентная система оценки, которая автоматизирует тестирование больших языковых моделей путём преобразования естественноязычных запросов в выполняемые рабочие процессы. Система включает компоненты для планирования бенчмарков, разрешения и нормализации датасетов, а также задаче-ориентированного выбора метрик и отчётности. Платформа интегрирует контрольные точки с участием человека для проверки и отката операций, сохраняя при этом трассировку данных для отладки и аудита. Экспериментально показано, что One-Eval эффективно выполняет комплексные оценки из различных естественноязычных запросов с минимальными усилиями пользователя.

en.title: Automating Language Model Evaluation with One-Eval
en.desc: One-Eval is an automated evaluation system designed for large language models, streamlining the assessment process by transforming natural language requests into executable workflows. It integrates several components, including NL2Bench for personalized benchmark planning and BenchResolve for automatic dataset handling and schema normalization. This system allows for customizable reporting and task-aware metric selection, enhancing the interpretability of evaluation results. Additionally, it includes human-in-the-loop checkpoints for oversight and maintains evidence trails for debugging, making evaluations more efficient and reproducible in practical applications.

zh.title: 自动化评估，简化语言模型评估流程
zh.desc: One-Eval 是一个自动化评估系统，旨在简化大型语言模型的评估过程。它通过将自然语言请求转换为可执行的工作流程，集成了基准规划、数据集处理和可定制报告功能。该系统使用 NL2Bench 进行意图结构化和个性化基准规划，同时通过 BenchResolve 处理基准解析和数据集获取。One-Eval 还支持人机协作的检查点，确保评估过程的可追溯性和可调试性，从而提高评估的效率和可重复性。

papers.title: Reliable Reasoning in SVG-LLMs via Multi-Task Multi-Reward Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract CTRL-S framework enhances SVG generation through chain-of-thought reasoning and multi-reward optimization, achieving better structural coherence and visual fidelity.  					AI-generated summary With the rapid advancement of vision-language models, an increasing number of studies have explored their potential for SVG generation tasks. Although existing approaches improve performance by constructing large-scale SVG datasets and introducing SVG-specific tokens, they still suffer from limited generalization, redundant paths in code outputs, and a lack of explicit reasoning. In this work, we present CTRL-S (Chain-of-Thought Reinforcement Learning for SVG), a unified framework that introduces a chain-of-thought mechanism to explicitly expose the model's reasoning process during SVG generation. To support this structured reasoning, we construct SVG-Sophia, a high-quality dataset containing 145K samples across SVG code refinement, Text-to-SVG, and Image-to-SVG tasks. By training the model to generate group-level structured SVG code, CTRL-S significantly improves structural coherence and visual fidelity. Furthermore, we adopt the GRPO algorithm and design a multi-reward optimization framework, incorporating DINO, image-text similarity, format, and code efficiency rewards. Through joint multi-reward optimization and multi-task training, our approach systematically enhances overall generation capabilities. Extensive experiments show that CTRL-S outperforms existing methods, achieving higher task success rates, superior SVG code quality, and exceptional visual fidelity.

ru.title: Цепочка рассуждений и множественные награды для совершенной генерации векторной графики
ru.desc: CTRL-S — это новая платформа для генерации SVG, которая использует механизм цепочки рассуждений для явного раскрытия процесса мышления модели во время создания векторной графики. Авторы построили высокачественный датасет SVG-Sophia с 145K образцами для различных задач преобразования. Фреймворк применяет алгоритм GRPO и мультивознаграждающую оптимизацию с использованием нескольких метрик оценки, включая качество кода и визуальную верность. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют превосходство CTRL-S над существующими методами в генерации структурированного SVG-кода и улучшении качества визуального вывода.

en.title: Enhancing SVG Generation with CTRL-S: Clear Reasoning and Multi-Reward Optimization
en.desc: The CTRL-S framework improves the generation of Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) by using a chain-of-thought reasoning approach and a multi-reward optimization strategy. This method allows the model to clearly demonstrate its reasoning process, which enhances the quality and coherence of the generated SVG code. Additionally, the framework is supported by the SVG-Sophia dataset, which contains a diverse range of SVG tasks, helping to train the model effectively. As a result, CTRL-S achieves better performance in terms of task success rates and visual fidelity compared to previous methods.

zh.title: CTRL-S：提升SVG生成的智能推理与优化
zh.desc: CTRL-S框架通过链式思维推理和多重奖励优化，提升了SVG生成的结构一致性和视觉保真度。该方法引入了链式思维机制，明确展示了模型在SVG生成过程中的推理过程。为了支持这种结构化推理，我们构建了SVG-Sophia数据集，包含145K个样本，涵盖SVG代码优化、文本到SVG和图像到SVG任务。通过联合多重奖励优化和多任务训练，CTRL-S显著提高了生成能力，实验结果表明其在任务成功率和SVG代码质量上优于现有方法。

papers.title: SegviGen: Repurposing 3D Generative Model for Part Segmentation
papers.abstract: Abstract SegviGen repurposes pretrained 3D generative models for efficient 3D part segmentation using distinctive part colorization, achieving superior performance with minimal labeled data.  					AI-generated summary We introduce SegviGen, a framework that repurposes native 3D generative models for 3D part segmentation. Existing pipelines either lift strong 2D priors into 3D via distillation or multi-view mask aggregation, often suffering from cross-view inconsistency and blurred boundaries, or explore native 3D discriminative segmentation, which typically requires large-scale annotated 3D data and substantial training resources. In contrast, SegviGen leverages the structured priors encoded in pretrained 3D generative model to induce segmentation through distinctive part colorization, establishing a novel and efficient framework for part segmentation. Specifically, SegviGen encodes a 3D asset and predicts part-indicative colors on active voxels of a geometry-aligned reconstruction. It supports interactive part segmentation, full segmentation, and full segmentation with 2D guidance in a unified framework. Extensive experiments show that SegviGen improves over the prior state of the art by 40% on interactive part segmentation and by 15% on full segmentation, while using only 0.32% of the labeled training data. It demonstrates that pretrained 3D generative priors transfer effectively to 3D part segmentation, enabling strong performance with limited supervision. See our project page at https://fenghora.github.io/SegviGen-Page/.

ru.title: Цветная роспись для понимания структуры 3D объектов
ru.desc: SegviGen — это фреймворк, который переиспользует предобученные 3D генеративные модели для задачи сегментации частей 3D объектов. Вместо традиционных подходов, которые либо переносят 2D признаки в 3D через дистилляцию, либо требуют больших объемов размеченных данных, метод использует структурированные приоры из генеративной модели и инициирует сегментацию через характерную окраску частей. Фреймворк поддерживает интерактивную сегментацию, полную сегментацию и сегментацию с 2D руководством в едином подходе. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что SegviGen превосходит существующие методы на 40% в интерактивной сегментации и на 15% в полной сегментации, используя при этом только 0,32% размеченных данных для обучения.

en.title: Efficient 3D Part Segmentation with Minimal Data
en.desc: SegviGen is a new framework that uses pretrained 3D generative models to improve the process of 3D part segmentation. Unlike traditional methods that often require large amounts of labeled data and can struggle with inconsistencies, SegviGen efficiently utilizes colorization to identify different parts of a 3D object. This approach allows for interactive and full segmentation while significantly reducing the need for extensive training resources. The results show that SegviGen outperforms previous methods, achieving better accuracy with only a small fraction of labeled data.

zh.title: SegviGen：高效的3D部件分割新方法
zh.desc: SegviGen 是一个将预训练的 3D 生成模型用于高效 3D 部件分割的框架。它通过独特的部件上色技术，利用预训练模型中编码的结构先验，实现了在标注数据极少的情况下，显著提升分割性能。与传统方法相比，SegviGen 避免了跨视图不一致和模糊边界的问题，提供了交互式分割和全分割的统一框架。实验结果表明，SegviGen 在交互式分割上提高了 40%，在全分割上提高了 15%，仅使用了 0.32% 的标注训练数据。

papers.title: SK-Adapter: Skeleton-Based Structural Control for Native 3D Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract SK-Adapter enables precise 3D structural control by treating skeletons as direct inputs through a lightweight adapter network that injects learnable tokens into frozen 3D generation models via cross-attention mechanisms.  					AI-generated summary Native 3D generative models have achieved remarkable fidelity and speed, yet they suffer from a critical limitation: inability to prescribe precise structural articulations, where precise structural control within the native 3D space remains underexplored. This paper proposes SK-Adapter, a simple and yet highly efficient and effective framework that unlocks precise skeletal manipulation for native 3D generation. Moving beyond text or image prompts, which can be ambiguous for precise structure, we treat the 3D skeleton as a first-class control signal. SK-Adapter is a lightweight structural adapter network that encodes joint coordinates and topology into learnable tokens, which are injected into the frozen 3D generation backbone via cross-attention. This smart design allows the model to not only effectively "attend" to specific 3D structural constraints but also preserve its original generative priors. To bridge the data gap, we contribute Objaverse-TMS dataset, a large-scale dataset of 24k text-mesh-skeleton pairs. Extensive experiments confirm that our method achieves robust structural control while preserving the geometry and texture quality of the foundation model, significantly outperforming existing baselines. Furthermore, we extend this capability to local 3D editing, enabling the region specific editing of existing assets with skeletal guidance, which is unattainable by previous methods. Project Page: https://sk-adapter.github.io/
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Скелет как точный язык управления 3D генерацией
ru.desc: SK-Adapter — это лёгкая адаптерная сеть, которая позволяет точно контролировать структуру 3D моделей, используя скелеты как прямые входные сигналы. Метод кодирует координаты суставов и топологию в обучаемые токены, которые внедряются в замороженную базовую модель генерации через механизм кросс-внимания. Авторы создали большой датасет Objaverse-TMS с 24k парами текста, меша и скелета для обучения. SK-Adapter обеспечивает точный структурный контроль при сохранении геометрии и текстур исходной модели, а также позволяет редактировать отдельные области существующих 3D объектов.

en.title: Unlocking Precise 3D Control with SK-Adapter
en.desc: The paper introduces SK-Adapter, a novel framework that enhances 3D generative models by allowing precise control over structural articulations using skeletons as direct inputs. It employs a lightweight adapter network that transforms joint coordinates and topology into learnable tokens, which are integrated into existing 3D generation models through cross-attention mechanisms. This approach not only maintains the original generative capabilities of the model but also enables effective manipulation of 3D structures, addressing a significant limitation in current models. Additionally, the authors present the Objaverse-TMS dataset, which consists of 24,000 text-mesh-skeleton pairs, to support their method and demonstrate its superior performance in local 3D editing tasks.

zh.title: SK-Adapter：精确控制3D结构的新方法
zh.desc: SK-Adapter是一种轻量级的适配器网络，通过交叉注意机制将可学习的标记注入到冻结的3D生成模型中，从而实现精确的3D结构控制。该方法将3D骨架视为一种重要的控制信号，超越了传统的文本或图像提示，解决了精确结构控制的不足。SK-Adapter能够有效地处理特定的3D结构约束，同时保持原始生成模型的特性。通过构建Objaverse-TMS数据集，作者验证了该方法在结构控制和局部3D编辑方面的优越性，显著超越了现有的基准。

papers.title: Recursive Language Models Meet Uncertainty: The Surprising Effectiveness of Self-Reflective Program Search for Long Context
papers.abstract: Abstract Language models struggle with long-context handling, but a new framework called SRLM improves performance by incorporating uncertainty-aware self-reflection to guide programmatic context interaction, outperforming existing methods like RLM across various tasks and context lengths.  					AI-generated summary Long-context handling remains a core challenge for language models: even with extended context windows, models often fail to reliably extract, reason over, and use the information across long contexts. Recent works like Recursive Language Models (RLM) have approached this challenge by agentic way of decomposing long contexts into recursive sub-calls through programmatic interaction at inference. While promising, the success of RLM critically depends on how these context-interaction programs are selected, which has remained largely unexplored. In this paper, we study this problem and introduce SRLM, a framework that augments programmatic context interaction with uncertainty-aware Self-Reflection. SRLM leverages three intrinsic signals: self consistency, reasoning length, and verbalized confidence. These serve as complementary indicators of a model's internal uncertainty, and the model uses them to evaluate and compare candidate context-interaction programs. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmark datasets, context lengths, and backbone models, show that SRLM consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, yielding up to 22% improvement over RLM under the same time budget. Our findings show that recursion itself is not the primary driver of performance in RLM, and a simple self-reflective program search can match or surpass RLM without requiring self-query or explicit recursion mechanisms. We find that for context lengths within the model's window, RLMs with recursion often degrade performance relative to the base model, whereas SRLM yields consistent gains across both short and long contexts. We also find that RLM is less effective in tasks with semantically intensive nature, where heuristic program search is insufficient and broader contextual understanding is required, while self-reflection in SRLM provides a semantic signal that better steers reasoning in these scenarios.

ru.title: Самосознание языковой модели как ключ к пониманию долгого контекста
ru.desc: В работе предложена система SRLM, которая решает проблему обработки длинного контекста в языковых моделях путём программного взаимодействия с контекстом, дополненного неопределённостью-осведомлённой саморефлексией. Система использует три внутренних сигнала: согласованность ответов, длину рассуждений и вербализованную уверенность модели для оценки и выбора оптимальных программ взаимодействия с контекстом. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что SRLM превосходит существующие методы, включая рекурсивные языковые модели, на 22% при одинаковом вычислительном бюджете. Подход демонстрирует особую эффективность на семантически сложных задачах, где саморефлексия обеспечивает лучшее направление процесса рассуждения по сравнению с простым поиском программ.

en.title: SRLM: Enhancing Long-Context Handling with Self-Reflection
en.desc: This paper introduces a new framework called SRLM that enhances how language models handle long contexts by using uncertainty-aware self-reflection. Unlike previous methods like Recursive Language Models (RLM), which rely on recursive sub-calls, SRLM evaluates context-interaction programs based on intrinsic signals of uncertainty. The framework shows significant improvements in performance, achieving up to 22% better results than RLM across various tasks and context lengths. The findings suggest that self-reflection is more effective than recursion for improving contextual understanding in language models.

zh.title: SRLM：提升长上下文处理的新框架
zh.desc: 语言模型在处理长上下文时面临挑战。SRLM框架通过引入不确定性感知的自我反思，改善了程序化上下文交互的性能。该框架利用自我一致性、推理长度和语言信心等信号，帮助模型评估和比较上下文交互程序。实验结果表明，SRLM在多种任务和上下文长度下均优于现有方法，提升了模型的整体表现。

papers.title: Learning Human-Object Interaction for 3D Human Pose Estimation from LiDAR Point Clouds
papers.abstract: Abstract Human-Object Interaction Learning framework addresses challenges in 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR point clouds by mitigating spatial ambiguity and class imbalance through contrastive learning and adaptive pooling techniques.  					AI-generated summary Understanding humans from LiDAR point clouds is one of the most critical tasks in autonomous driving due to its close relationships with pedestrian safety, yet it remains challenging in the presence of diverse human-object interactions and cluttered backgrounds. Nevertheless, existing methods largely overlook the potential of leveraging human-object interactions to build robust 3D human pose estimation frameworks. There are two major challenges that motivate the incorporation of human-object interaction. First, human-object interactions introduce spatial ambiguity between human and object points, which often leads to erroneous 3D human keypoint predictions in interaction regions. Second, there exists severe class imbalance in the number of points between interacting and non-interacting body parts, with the interaction-frequent regions such as hand and foot being sparsely observed in LiDAR data. To address these challenges, we propose a Human-Object Interaction Learning (HOIL) framework for robust 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR point clouds. To mitigate the spatial ambiguity issue, we present human-object interaction-aware contrastive learning (HOICL) that effectively enhances feature discrimination between human and object points, particularly in interaction regions. To alleviate the class imbalance issue, we introduce contact-aware part-guided pooling (CPPool) that adaptively reallocates representational capacity by compressing overrepresented points while preserving informative points from interacting body parts. In addition, we present an optional contact-based temporal refinement that refines erroneous per-frame keypoint estimates using contact cues over time. As a result, our HOIL effectively leverages human-object interaction to resolve spatial ambiguity and class imbalance in interaction regions. Codes will be released.
affiliations: Dept. of ECE&ASRI, Seoul National University

ru.title: Взаимодействие человека и объектов для надёжной оценки позы в облаках точек
ru.desc: В работе предложена структура HOIL для оценки трёхмерной позы человека из облаков точек LiDAR, которая учитывает взаимодействие человека с объектами. Авторы решают проблему пространственной неоднозначности между точками человека и объекта с помощью контрастного обучения, чувствительного к взаимодействиям. Для борьбы с дисбалансом классов (редкие точки в областях рук и ног) используется адаптивное пулинг с учётом контакта, которое перераспределяет представительскую способность признаков. Дополнительно применяется временное уточнение на основе контактных сигналов между кадрами для повышения точности оценки позы.

en.title: Enhancing 3D Human Pose Estimation through Human-Object Interaction
en.desc: The Human-Object Interaction Learning (HOIL) framework improves 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR point clouds by addressing spatial ambiguity and class imbalance. It uses contrastive learning to enhance the distinction between human and object points, especially in areas where they interact. Additionally, it employs adaptive pooling techniques to manage the uneven distribution of data points, ensuring that important features from interacting body parts are preserved. This approach ultimately leads to more accurate predictions of human poses in complex environments, which is crucial for applications like autonomous driving.

zh.title: 人-物交互助力3D姿态估计
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种人-物交互学习框架，旨在解决从LiDAR点云中进行3D人类姿态估计的挑战。该框架通过对比学习和自适应池化技术，减轻了空间模糊性和类别不平衡的问题。具体来说，采用人-物交互感知的对比学习方法，提高了人类和物体点之间的特征区分能力。通过接触感知的部分引导池化，框架有效地重新分配了表示能力，从而改善了交互区域的关键点预测。

papers.title: Polyglot-Lion: Efficient Multilingual ASR for Singapore via Balanced Fine-Tuning of Qwen3-ASR
papers.abstract: Abstract Polyglot-Lion, a compact multilingual ASR model family for Singapore's linguistic diversity, achieves competitive performance with significantly reduced training cost and improved inference speed through balanced fine-tuning of pretrained models.  					AI-generated summary We present Polyglot-Lion, a family of compact multilingual automatic speech recognition (ASR) models tailored for the linguistic landscape of Singapore, covering English, Mandarin, Tamil, and Malay. Our models are obtained by fine-tuning Qwen3-ASR-0.6B and Qwen3-ASR-1.7B exclusively on publicly available speech corpora, using a balanced sampling strategy that equalizes the number of training utterances per language and deliberately omits language-tag conditioning so that the model learns to identify languages implicitly from audio. On 12 benchmarks spanning the four target languages, Polyglot-Lion-1.7B achieves an average error rate of 14.85, competitive with MERaLiON-2-10B-ASR (14.32) - a model 6x larger - while incurring a training cost of \81 on a single RTX PRO 6000 GPU compared to 18,862 for the 128-GPU baseline. Inference throughput is approximately 20x faster than MERaLiON at 0.10 s/sample versus 2.02 s/sample. These results demonstrate that linguistically balanced fine-tuning of moderate-scale pretrained models can yield deployment-ready multilingual ASR at a fraction of the cost of larger specialist systems.

ru.title: Компактные мультиязычные модели распознавания речи через сбалансированный fine-tuning
ru.desc: В статье представлена Polyglot-Lion — семейство компактных мультиязычных моделей распознавания речи (ASR), разработанных для многоязычного ландшафта Сингапура с поддержкой английского, мандарина, тамильского и малайского языков. Модели получены путём fine-tuning предобученных моделей Qwen3-ASR на открытых речевых корпусах с использованием сбалансированной стратегии выборки и без явного кодирования языка, позволяя модели неявно определять язык из аудиосигнала. Polyglot-Lion-1.7B достигает конкурентоспособной частоты ошибок (14.85) при 6-кратном уменьшении размера модели и 230-кратном снижении стоимости обучения по сравнению с базовой системой. Предложенный подход демонстрирует, что сбалансированный fine-tuning модерируемых предобученных моделей позволяет создавать эффективные мультиязычные ASR-системы с минимальными вычислительными затратами.

en.title: Efficient Multilingual ASR for Singapore's Linguistic Diversity
en.desc: The paper introduces Polyglot-Lion, a series of compact multilingual automatic speech recognition (ASR) models designed for Singapore's diverse languages, including English, Mandarin, Tamil, and Malay. These models are fine-tuned from existing pretrained models using a balanced sampling method that ensures equal representation of each language in the training data. The results show that Polyglot-Lion-1.7B achieves a competitive error rate while being significantly smaller and cheaper to train than larger models. Additionally, it offers much faster inference speeds, demonstrating the effectiveness of balanced fine-tuning for multilingual ASR applications.

zh.title: 平衡微调，低成本多语言识别
zh.desc: Polyglot-Lion是一种针对新加坡语言多样性的紧凑型多语言自动语音识别（ASR）模型系列。该模型通过对预训练模型进行平衡微调，显著降低了训练成本并提高了推理速度。模型在英语、普通话、泰米尔语和马来语等四种语言上表现出色，平均错误率为14.85，接近一个六倍更大的模型。研究表明，适度规模的预训练模型经过语言平衡微调后，可以以较低的成本实现多语言ASR的部署。

papers.title: FlashSampling: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Sampling
papers.abstract: Abstract FlashSampling enables efficient categorical sampling by fusing the operation into the language model head matmul, eliminating memory overhead and reducing decoding time by up to 19%.  					AI-generated summary Sampling from a categorical distribution is mathematically simple, but in large-vocabulary decoding, it often triggers extra memory traffic and extra kernels after the LM head. We present FlashSampling, an exact sampling primitive that fuses sampling into the LM-head matmul and never materializes the logits tensor in HBM. The method is simple: compute logits tile-by-tile on chip, add Gumbel noise, keep only one maximizer per row and per vocabulary tile, and finish with a small reduction over tiles. The fused tiled kernel is exact because argmax decomposes over a partition; grouped variants for online and tensor-parallel settings are exact by hierarchical factorization of the categorical distribution. Across H100, H200, B200, and B300 GPUs, FlashSampling speeds up kernel-level decode workloads, and in end-to-end vLLM experiments, it reduces time per output token by up to 19% on the models we test. These results show that exact sampling, with no approximation, can be integrated into the matmul itself, turning a bandwidth-bound postprocessing step into a lightweight epilogue. Project Page: https://github.com/FlashSampling/FlashSampling.

ru.title: Семплирование внутри матрицы: от узкого места к молниеносной скорости
ru.desc: FlashSampling — это новый метод для эффективного категориального семплирования при декодировании языковых моделей. Вместо отдельного вычисления полного тензора логитов, метод встраивает семплирование прямо в операцию матричного умножения LM head, что исключает необходимость в дополнительной памяти. Алгоритм работает поблочно на чипе, добавляет шум Гумбеля и сохраняет только максимум для каждой строки, затем выполняет финальную редукцию. На практике FlashSampling сокращает время декодирования до 19% без какой-либо потери точности.

en.title: Speed Up Language Model Decoding with FlashSampling!
en.desc: FlashSampling is a novel technique that enhances categorical sampling in language models by integrating it directly into the matrix multiplication (matmul) operation of the model's head. This approach eliminates the need for additional memory usage and reduces decoding time significantly, achieving up to a 19% speedup. The method involves computing logits in smaller tiles, applying Gumbel noise, and selecting the maximum value efficiently without generating the full logits tensor. By optimizing the sampling process, FlashSampling transforms a traditionally bandwidth-heavy operation into a more efficient and streamlined step in the decoding pipeline.

zh.title: FlashSampling：高效的分类采样新方法
zh.desc: FlashSampling是一种高效的分类采样方法，它将采样操作与语言模型的矩阵乘法（matmul）融合在一起，从而消除了内存开销，并将解码时间减少了多达19%。该方法通过逐块计算logits，添加Gumbel噪声，并在每行和每个词汇块中仅保留一个最大值，最后对块进行小规模的归约。FlashSampling的融合核是精确的，因为argmax在分区上是可分解的，适用于在线和张量并行设置的分组变体通过分层因式分解分类分布实现精确性。实验结果表明，FlashSampling在多个GPU上加速了内核级解码工作负载，并在端到端的vLLM实验中显著提高了输出令牌的处理速度。

papers.title: CCTU: A Benchmark for Tool Use under Complex Constraints
papers.abstract: Abstract  Solving problems through tool use under explicit constraints constitutes a highly challenging yet unavoidable scenario for large language models (LLMs), requiring capabilities such as function calling, instruction following, and self-refinement. However, progress has been hindered by the absence of dedicated evaluations. To address this, we introduce CCTU, a benchmark for evaluating LLM tool use under complex constraints. CCTU is grounded in a taxonomy of 12 constraint categories spanning four dimensions (i.e., resource, behavior, toolset, and response). The benchmark comprises 200 carefully curated and challenging test cases across diverse tool-use scenarios, each involving an average of seven constraint types and an average prompt length exceeding 4,700 tokens. To enable reliable evaluation, we develop an executable constraint validation module that performs step-level validation and enforces compliance during multi-turn interactions between models and their environments. We evaluate nine state-of-the-art LLMs in both thinking and non-thinking modes. Results indicate that when strict adherence to all constraints is required, no model achieves a task completion rate above 20%. Further analysis reveals that models violate constraints in over 50% of cases, particularly in the resource and response dimensions. Moreover, LLMs demonstrate limited capacity for self-refinement even after receiving detailed feedback on constraint violations, highlighting a critical bottleneck in the development of robust tool-use agents. To facilitate future research, we release the data and code.
affiliations: College of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence, Fudan University

ru.title: Когда LLM с инструментами не могут следовать правилам
ru.desc: В статье представлена CCTU — бенчмарк для оценки способности больших языковых моделей использовать инструменты при наличии сложных ограничений. Авторы разработали таксономию из 12 категорий ограничений по четырём измерениям (ресурсы, поведение, набор инструментов и формат ответа) и создали 200 тестовых примеров с средней длиной промпта более 4700 токенов. Для надёжной оценки реализован модуль валидации ограничений, который проверяет соответствие на каждом шаге взаимодействия между моделью и окружением. Результаты показывают, что при строгом соблюдении всех ограничений ни одна из девяти протестированных LLM не достигает 20% успешных завершений задач, что указывает на критические проблемы в разработке надёжных агентов с использованием инструментов.

en.title: CCTU: Benchmarking Tool Use Under Constraints for LLMs
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges faced by large language models (LLMs) when using tools under strict constraints. It introduces CCTU, a benchmark designed to evaluate LLM performance in complex scenarios involving multiple constraints. The benchmark includes 200 test cases that require models to navigate various resource, behavior, toolset, and response constraints. The findings reveal that current LLMs struggle significantly with constraint adherence, achieving low task completion rates and frequently violating constraints, indicating a need for improved self-refinement capabilities.

zh.title: 评估大型语言模型工具使用能力的新基准
zh.desc: 本文介绍了CCTU，一个用于评估大型语言模型（LLMs）在复杂约束下工具使用能力的基准。CCTU基于12个约束类别的分类法，涵盖资源、行为、工具集和响应四个维度，包含200个精心策划的测试案例。研究表明，当要求严格遵守所有约束时，没有模型的任务完成率超过20%，且模型在超过50%的情况下违反约束，尤其是在资源和响应维度。此研究揭示了LLMs在自我改进能力方面的局限性，并为未来的研究提供了数据和代码。

papers.title: Efficient Reasoning on the Edge
papers.abstract: Abstract Lightweight reasoning in small language models is enabled through LoRA adapters, budget forcing via reinforcement learning, parallel test-time scaling, and dynamic adapter switching under strict resource constraints.  					AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) with chain-of-thought reasoning achieve state-of-the-art performance across complex problem-solving tasks, but their verbose reasoning traces and large context requirements make them impractical for edge deployment. These challenges include high token generation costs, large KV-cache footprints, and inefficiencies when distilling reasoning capabilities into smaller models for mobile devices. Existing approaches often rely on distilling reasoning traces from larger models into smaller models, which are verbose and stylistically redundant, undesirable for on-device inference. In this work, we propose a lightweight approach to enable reasoning in small LLMs using LoRA adapters combined with supervised fine-tuning. We further introduce budget forcing via reinforcement learning on these adapters, significantly reducing response length with minimal accuracy loss. To address memory-bound decoding, we exploit parallel test-time scaling, improving accuracy at minor latency increase. Finally, we present a dynamic adapter-switching mechanism that activates reasoning only when needed and a KV-cache sharing strategy during prompt encoding, reducing time-to-first-token for on-device inference. Experiments on Qwen2.5-7B demonstrate that our method achieves efficient, accurate reasoning under strict resource constraints, making LLM reasoning practical for mobile scenarios. Videos demonstrating our solution running on mobile devices are available on our project page.

ru.title: Лёгкие рассуждения в компактных моделях: от облака на мобильник
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод включения цепочки рассуждений в маленькие языковые модели с использованием LoRA адаптеров и контролируемой тонкой настройки. Авторы применяют reinforcement learning для ограничения длины ответов, что значительно сокращает количество генерируемых токенов без существенной потери качества. Для решения проблемы памяти при декодировании используется параллельное масштабирование на этапе тестирования и динамическое переключение между адаптерами. Решение позволяет эффективно запускать логическое рассуждение на мобильных устройствах с ограниченными ресурсами, достигая практической применимости LLM на edge-девайсах.

en.title: Efficient Reasoning for Small Language Models on Mobile Devices
en.desc: This paper presents a method to enhance reasoning capabilities in small language models using LoRA adapters and reinforcement learning. The approach focuses on reducing the verbosity of reasoning outputs while maintaining accuracy, making it suitable for mobile devices. By implementing parallel test-time scaling and dynamic adapter switching, the method optimizes memory usage and response times. The results show that this lightweight reasoning technique allows for effective on-device inference under strict resource constraints.

zh.title: 轻量级推理，移动设备的理想选择
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种轻量级的方法，使小型语言模型能够进行推理，使用了LoRA适配器和监督微调相结合的技术。通过强化学习实现预算强制，显著减少了响应长度，同时保持了较小的准确性损失。我们还利用并行测试时间扩展来解决内存限制解码的问题，提高了准确性，延迟增加很小。最后，动态适配器切换机制在需要时激活推理，并在提示编码期间共享KV缓存，减少了设备推理的首次响应时间。

papers.title: MolmoB0T: Large-Scale Simulation Enables Zero-Shot Manipulation
papers.abstract: Abstract Zero-shot sim-to-real transfer is demonstrated for robotic manipulation using large-scale synthetic data and vision-language models with flow-matching action heads, achieving high success rates without real-world fine-tuning.  					AI-generated summary A prevailing view in robot learning is that simulation alone is not enough; effective sim-to-real transfer is widely believed to require at least some real-world data collection or task-specific fine-tuning to bridge the gap between simulated and physical environments. We challenge that assumption. With sufficiently large-scale and diverse simulated synthetic training data, we show that zero-shot transfer to the real world is not only possible, but effective for both static and mobile manipulation. We introduce MolmoBot-Engine, a fully open-source pipeline for procedural data generation across robots, tasks, and diverse simulated environments in MolmoSpaces. With it, we release MolmoBot-Data, a dataset of 1.8 million expert trajectories for articulated object manipulation and pick-and-place tasks. We train three policy classes: MolmoBot, a Molmo2-based multi-frame vision-language model with a flow-matching action head; MolmoBot-Pi0, which replicates the π_0 architecture to enable direct comparison; and MolmoBot-SPOC, a lightweight policy suitable for edge deployment and amenable to RL fine-tuning. We evaluate on two robotic platforms: the Franka FR3 for tabletop manipulation tasks and the Rainbow Robotics RB-Y1 mobile manipulator for door opening, drawer manipulation, cabinet interaction, and mobile pick-and-place. Without any real-world fine-tuning, our policies achieve zero-shot transfer to unseen objects and environments. On tabletop pick-and-place, MolmoBot achieves a success rate of 79.2% in real world evaluations across 4 settings, outperforming π_{0.5} at 39.2%. Our results demonstrate that procedural environment generation combined with diverse articulated assets can produce robust manipulation policies that generalize broadly to the real world. Technical Blog: https://allenai.org/blog/molmobot-robot-manipulation

ru.title: От симуляции к реальности: нулевая подгонка для манипуляторов
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали подход для обучения роботов манипуляции без использования данных из реального мира. Они создали MolmoBot-Engine — систему для процедурной генерации синтетических данных и собрали датасет из 1.8 миллионов траекторий экспертов для обучения политик управления роботом. На основе vision-language модели Molmo2 с flow-matching головой для предсказания действий были обучены три класса политик, которые успешно переносятся на реальные роботов без дополнительной подгонки. Результаты показали, что MolmoBot достигает 79.2% успеха при выполнении задач манипуляции в реальном мире, значительно превосходя существующие подходы.

en.title: Zero-Shot Transfer: Robots Learn to Manipulate Without Real-World Data!
en.desc: This paper presents a method for zero-shot sim-to-real transfer in robotic manipulation, showing that robots can effectively operate in the real world using only synthetic data. The authors introduce the MolmoBot-Engine, an open-source framework for generating diverse training data, which includes a dataset of 1.8 million expert trajectories for various manipulation tasks. They train multiple policy models, including a vision-language model with flow-matching action heads, achieving high success rates in real-world tasks without any fine-tuning. The results indicate that with sufficient synthetic training data, robots can generalize their learned skills to new, unseen environments and objects effectively.

zh.title: 零-shot转移：模拟与现实的无缝连接
zh.desc: 这篇论文展示了零-shot的模拟到现实转移在机器人操作中的应用，使用大规模合成数据和视觉-语言模型，成功率高且无需在现实世界中进行微调。研究挑战了传统观点，认为仅靠模拟数据无法有效转移到现实环境。通过足够大规模和多样化的合成训练数据，研究表明零-shot转移不仅可行，而且在静态和移动操作中都有效。论文还介绍了MolmoBot-Engine，这是一个开放源代码的程序数据生成管道，支持多种机器人和任务的操作。

papers.title: OneWorld: Taming Scene Generation with 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder
papers.abstract: Abstract OneWorld enables 3D scene generation by performing diffusion in a unified 3D representation space using a 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder and specialized consistency losses.  					AI-generated summary Existing diffusion-based 3D scene generation methods primarily operate in 2D image/video latent spaces, which makes maintaining cross-view appearance and geometric consistency inherently challenging. To bridge this gap, we present OneWorld, a framework that performs diffusion directly within a coherent 3D representation space. Central to our approach is the 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE); it leverages pretrained 3D foundation models and augments their geometry-centric nature by injecting appearance and distilling semantics into a unified 3D latent space. Furthermore, we introduce token-level Cross-View-Correspondence (CVC) consistency loss to explicitly enforce structural alignment across views, and propose Manifold-Drift Forcing (MDF) to mitigate train-inference exposure bias and shape a robust 3D manifold by mixing drifted and original representations. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that OneWorld generates high-quality 3D scenes with superior cross-view consistency compared to state-of-the-art 2D-based methods. Our code will be available at https://github.com/SensenGao/OneWorld.
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence

ru.title: Диффузия в едином 3D пространстве для согласованной генерации сцен
ru.desc: OneWorld представляет новый подход к генерации 3D сцен, выполняя диффузию прямо в единого 3D представлении вместо традиционных 2D латентных пространств. Ключевой компонент — 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE), который объединяет претренированные 3D модели-основы и вводит информацию о внешнем виде и семантике в единое 3D латентное пространство. Авторы предлагают потоковую потерю Cross-View-Correspondence (CVC) для явного обеспечения согласованности между видами и Manifold-Drift Forcing (MDF) для уменьшения смещения экспозиции обучения-инференса. Эксперименты показывают, что метод генерирует высококачественные 3D сцены с превосходной межвидовой согласованностью в сравнении с современными 2D-методами.

en.title: OneWorld: Bridging 3D Scene Generation with Unified Diffusion
en.desc: OneWorld is a novel framework for generating 3D scenes by utilizing diffusion processes within a unified 3D representation space. It employs a 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE) that enhances existing 3D models by integrating appearance and semantic information into a single latent space. The framework introduces a token-level Cross-View-Correspondence (CVC) consistency loss to ensure that different views of the scene maintain structural alignment. Additionally, it incorporates Manifold-Drift Forcing (MDF) to reduce biases during training and improve the robustness of the generated 3D representations.

zh.title: 统一3D表示，生成高质量场景
zh.desc: OneWorld 是一个用于生成 3D 场景的框架，它通过在统一的 3D 表示空间中进行扩散来实现。该方法使用了 3D 统一表示自编码器（3D-URAE），并结合了预训练的 3D 基础模型，以增强几何特性并注入外观信息。为了确保不同视角之间的结构一致性，OneWorld 引入了基于标记的跨视角对应一致性损失（CVC）。此外，提出的流形漂移强制（MDF）方法可以减少训练和推理之间的偏差，从而生成高质量且视角一致的 3D 场景。

papers.title: Measuring Primitive Accumulation: An Information-Theoretic Approach to Capitalist Enclosure in PIK2, Indonesia
papers.abstract: Abstract  Large-scale land enclosure for speculative mega-development constitutes a non-equilibrium spatial process whose velocity, topology, and irreversibility remain poorly quantified. We study the Pantai Indah Kapuk 2 (PIK2) coastal mega-development north of Jakarta, Indonesia, using eight years (2017--2024) of Sentinel-2 land-use/land-cover (LULC) data at 10-meter resolution. The landscape is projected onto a Marxian probability simplex partitioning terrestrial pixels into Commons, Agrarian, and Capital fractions. Fisher-Rao (FR) geodesic distances on this simplex identify a transformation pulse of 0.405~rad/yr during 2019--2020, coinciding with major construction activity. Absorbing Markov chain analysis yields expected absorption times into the built environment of 46.0~years for cropland and 38.1~years for tree cover, with a pooled built-area self-retention rate of 96.4%. Percolation analysis reveals that a giant connected component containing 89--95% of all built pixels persists at occupation probabilities p in [0.096, 0.162], far below the random percolation threshold p_c approx 0.593, indicating planned rather than stochastic spatial growth. The box-counting fractal dimension of the urban boundary increases from d_f = 1.316 to 1.397, consistent with increasingly irregular frontier expansion. These results suggest that information-geometric and statistical-mechanical tools can characterize the kinematic and topological signatures of capitalist spatial accumulation with quantitative precision.

ru.title: Геометрия земельного захвата: количественный анализ необратимой урбанизации
ru.desc: Исследование использует данные спутниковых снимков Sentinel-2 для анализа крупномасштабного освоения земель в индонезийском мегапроекте PIK2. Авторы применяют информационную геометрию и марковские цепи для количественной оценки необратимого преобразования ландшафта из сельскохозяйственных угодий в застроенную среду. Анализ показывает, что урбанизация происходит по планомерной схеме, а не случайно, с характерным временем поглощения естественных экосистем в пределах 40 лет. Фрактальный анализ границ городского развития выявляет усложнение пространственной структуры, что свидетельствует о геометрически упорядоченном характере капиталистического накопления в пространстве.

en.title: Quantifying Urban Growth: The Science of Planned Development
en.desc: This paper investigates the rapid development of the Pantai Indah Kapuk 2 area in Indonesia using advanced machine learning techniques on land-use data. It employs Fisher-Rao geodesic distances to analyze the transformation of land over time, particularly during significant construction phases. The study also utilizes absorbing Markov chain analysis to predict how long it will take for different land types to be converted into built environments. Additionally, it applies percolation theory to demonstrate that the urban growth is planned rather than random, highlighting the structured nature of capitalist land development.

zh.title: 量化资本主义空间积累的动态特征
zh.desc: 这篇论文研究了印度尼西亚雅加达北部的Pantai Indah Kapuk 2（PIK2）沿海大型开发项目，使用了2017年至2024年的Sentinel-2土地利用/土地覆盖数据。研究表明，该地区的空间发展过程具有非平衡特征，且其速度和拓扑结构尚未得到充分量化。通过Fisher-Rao几何距离和吸收马尔可夫链分析，论文揭示了土地转变的时间和模式，显示出计划性而非随机的空间增长。结果表明，信息几何和统计力学工具能够精确地表征资本主义空间积累的运动学和拓扑特征。
